recap:
- Developers choose between Code-First and Declarative methods when building AI agents on Databricks.
- Code-First allows maximum control with manual coding of agent logic using frameworks like Mosaic AI, but involves higher technical debt and manual optimization.
- In Code-First, developers manage retrieval logic, prompt templates, embedding models, and vector search synchronization, requiring code rewrites for changes.
- Declarative methods focus on outcomes, allowing developers to specify what the agent should do without detailing how to achieve it.
- In Declarative, tools like Agent Bricks automate tasks such as parsing, indexing, and prompt engineering, leading to faster deployment and auto-optimization.
- Code-First offers infinite customizability, while Declarative provides quicker time-to-value but less granular control over execution logic.
